scenarios.query:
Review this PR:
```text
Title: "stuff + fixes"
Description: (empty)
Diff: +1,850 −400 across 31 files: new SSO login flow, plus a global
  tabs->spaces reformat, plus renaming all *Util classes to *Helper
Commits: "asdf", "works now", "final", "final2"
Leftover in diff: print("DEBUG user=", user), aws_keys.json committed
```
expected_behavior: Flags the oversized mixed-purpose diff (feature + reformat + rename) and recommends splitting
Flags the meaningless commit messages preventing revert/bisect and the empty description with no risk/rollback/test notes
Flags the committed credentials file as requiring removal AND rotation, and the debug print leftover

scenarios.query:
Review this PR:
```text
Title: "refactor: simplify webhook payloads"
Commits: refactor: simplify webhook payloads
Diff: removes the `legacy_id` field from all outgoing webhook payloads;
  consumers were emailed last month.
Changelog: no entry. Docs: webhook reference still lists legacy_id.
```
expected_behavior: Flags the breaking change mistyped as refactor with no BREAKING CHANGE signal (it alters a public contract and drives versioning)
Flags the missing changelog entry and the stale webhook docs still listing the removed field
Recommends a migration note/deprecation signaling in the PR itself, not just an email

scenarios.query:
Review this PR:
```text
Title: "fix: retry webhook delivery on 503 with backoff (#1204)"
Description: root cause (provider maintenance windows), fix (3 retries,
  exponential backoff), blast radius (webhook sender only), tested: unit +
  staging soak, rollback: revert, flag WEBHOOK_RETRY kill-switch included.
Diff: +61 −9 in 4 files (fix, tests, changelog, runbook note)
Commits: one, imperative, explains why, links #1204
```
expected_behavior: Recognizes the small single-purpose diff, correct typing, complete description with rollback, and docs/changelog in the same PR
Does not demand extra process ceremony for an already well-hygiened small fix

scenarios.query: Review this PR's description against its diff. Description: 'Fixes the rounding bug in invoice totals (closes #812). Also a pure refactor of the tax module — no behavior change. ~30% faster.' Diff: changes `round(x, 2)` to integer-cent math in billing.py (no new test); renames functions in tax.py AND changes one conditional from `>=` to `>`; no benchmark anywhere.
expected_behavior: Flags 'closes #812' with no regression test that would fail without the fix — claim unsupported by evidence in the diff
Flags the 'pure refactor / no behavior change' claim as false: the `>=`→`>` change in tax.py is a behavior change smuggled into a rename
Flags the '~30% faster' claim as having no benchmark or profile — an unsupported performance claim is itself a finding
